methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $6.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $3.3B - $5.4B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Form Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series G), post-money $3.5B

data.narrative:
TAE Technologies is a Series G climate and energy company with a last post-money valuation of $6.0B and cumulative fundraising exceeding $1.3B. Valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 9 private and 1 public frontier-tech company in the same sector and comparable or adjacent stages, including peers such as Helion ($5.4B, Series F), Form Energy ($3.5B, Series G), and Sila Nanotechnologies ($3.3B, Late Stage).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.25x step-up multiplier to the $6.0B last post-money valuation, implying $7.5B. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median of $3.5B, with a range of $3.3B to $5.4B. These methods capture both the trajectory assumption implicit in late-stage progression and the empirical median settlement of recently-valued peers.

Triangulated analysis across these two methods supports a valuation range of $3.0B (low) to $8.6B (high), with a median of $5.5B. The current $6.0B valuation sits in the upper-middle of this range and above the peer median, reflecting either TAE's differentiated position relative to sampled comparables or potential compression risk to peer-median levels. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 3-peer sample and the applicability of stage-progression multipliers in the frontier-energy sector.
